response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number_of_uses] ounces of shampoo in a leap year. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number_of_times] times during that period. Multiplying the number of uses by the amount of shampoo used each time gives the total amount of shampoo needed. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, dividing the total amount needed by 14 will tell us how many bottles she needs to buy.

To find the exact number of bottles, first calculate the total amount of shampoo used in a leap year, which is [total_ounces_needed]. Then, divide this by the size of each bottle, which is [bottle_size], to determine how many bottles she must purchase. If the result is not a whole number, she will need to round up to ensure she has enough shampoo for the entire year.
